news.title: Delhi: DDA informs NGT it will complete O zone drone survey in two months
news.para: Delhi Development Authority will finish a drone survey of the Yamuna floodplain in two months. This survey covers 165 square kilometers. DDA has already removed encroachments from seven acres. The survey is part of efforts to document the O zone. Authorities are monitoring for further illegal constructions. Police have been directed to prevent unauthorized building within the zone.

news.title: Andhra Pradesh government offers 50% VLT relief to boost construction activity
news.para: Property owners in the state will now benefit from a 50% discount on vacant land tax during construction. This policy shift, effective from construction commencement until occupancy certificate issuance, aims to stimulate building activity and alleviate financial pressure. Officials emphasized this move will encourage timely project completion and better land utilization across urban areas.

news.title: Telangana launches first land registration with survey map under Bhu Bharati
news.para: On Tuesday, the first land registration with a survey map was completed in Kusumanchi mandal of Khammam district. Responding to the milestone, Telangana revenue minister Ponguleti Srinivas Reddy said that the integrated Bhu Bharati portal would emerge as a model for the nation.

news.title: Cash-strapped Pune Cantonment plans commercial makeover of 120-year-old MG Road building
news.para: Pune Cantonment Board is set to transform its old headquarters on MG Road into a commercial complex. This move aims to generate much-needed revenue for the board. The colonial-era building, vacant since June 2024, will be redeveloped. This initiative is expected to boost the board's finances significantly. The project awaits final approval from higher authorities.

news.title: ReaRCo acquires KV Developers through the insolvency process
news.para: ReaRCo Private Limited has acquired KV Developers, bringing relief to nearly 400 homebuyers in Greater Noida. The company secured Rs 195 crore from the SWAMIH Fund to finish the stalled project. Construction is underway on five towers, with possession expected by year-end. Dues to financial institutions and the development authority have been cleared.

news.title: Telangana's TDR policy sparks price surge, Hyderabad builders cry foul
news.para: Builders, developers, and ordinary property owners are now raising concerns about what they describe as an artificial price surge and possible market manipulation. TDR is a certificate issued to landowners who surrender property for public purposes such as road widening, the Musi rejuvenation project, and other infrastructure initiatives.

news.title: Gurugram: GMDA issues notice to builder as road obstructs drain in Ramprastha City
news.para: GMDA has issued a show-cause notice to the developer of Ramprastha City for constructing an unauthorized access obstructing the Badshapur stormwater drain. The authority warned of demolition and legal action if the developer fails to provide a satisfactory reply within seven days, citing potential waterlogging and infrastructure damage.

news.title: UK's British Land lifts earnings forecast as AI firms flock to London campuses
news.para: AI and tech firms are fueling double-digit rental growth at British Land's London campuses, with Anthropic signing its sixth lease. This surge in demand from innovation-led occupiers has prompted British Land to raise its earnings guidance for fiscal years 2025 and 2027, highlighting London's growing importance as a tech hub.

news.title: Kalamassery municipality issues draft notification for changes to city structural plan
news.para: The amendment published by Kerala govt on March 15, 2026, aims to provide immediate relief to residents and developers in the municipalities and panchayats surrounding the corporation until the comprehensive 2040 Amrut Master Plan is fully implemented.

news.title: NCR workers' wage hike may push up living costs in gated societies
news.para: Rising minimum wages and worker unrest in the NCR are poised to increase living costs in gated communities and inflate construction expenses. Facility management firms will likely pass higher labor costs to residents via maintenance charges, while developers face challenges absorbing these pressures due to already slowed home sales.

news.title: Average office rentals in Delhi-NCR, Bengaluru cross ₹100 per sq ft per month: Knight Frank
news.para: Real estate consultant Knight Frank data showed that the average rentals for office spaces rose in a range of 2-15 per cent during January-March 2026 across eight major cities of India compared to the same period last year. The maximum annual growth of 15 per cent in office rent was seen in Delhi-NCR and Kolkata.

news.title: Manasum Senior Living plans to invest ₹150 crore to develop senior living project in Chennai
news.para: Manasum Senior Living is investing ₹150 crore in Chennai for The Madras Pavilion, a senior living project. This development will feature 92 villas and 36 apartments. The company also plans a ₹550 crore investment to expand its operations across South and West India.

news.title: Vadodara civic body's built homes fall apart in 20 years
news.para: Residents of Vadodara's JNNURM housing schemes are experiencing poor living conditions. Houses built for the economically weaker sections are showing signs of damage. Issues like water seepage and falling plaster have been reported. The Vadodara Municipal Corporation states the core structures are sound. They attribute problems to seepage and neglect by residents.

news.title: Karnataka HC says sub-registrars must update documents within four weeks
news.para: Karnataka High Court has issued new guidelines for civil and commercial courts and sub-registrars.  These rules aim to speed up the updating of property documents when sale deeds are declared void.  Courts must send decrees to sub-registrars within four weeks. Sub-registrars must then update records promptly. This initiative seeks to prevent unnecessary legal battles and save valuable court time.

news.title: Gurugram civic body plans to seal properties, auction assets over unpaid GRAP challans
news.para: Gurugram Municipal Corporation is launching a strict recovery campaign for unpaid fines issued during GRAP implementation. Officials face accountability for collecting dues. Property sealing and auctions are threatened for non-payment. The corporation is also focusing on efficient project planning and improving public grievance redressal systems. Complaint resolution will be reviewed weekly.

news.title: Government land worth ₹30 crore freed from encroachment, 12 houses demolished in Indore
news.para: Indore district administration launched a significant anti-encroachment drive, reclaiming prime government land valued at approximately Rs 30 crore. Following collector Shivam Verma's orders, authorities demolished 12 illegally constructed houses spanning 30,000 square feet in Khajrana village. The administration has vowed to continue strict action against land encroachers.

news.title: Global tensions simmer, but real estate market stable in Bengaluru
news.para: Bengaluru's real estate market remains stable despite global uncertainties, with strong local factors like job growth and infrastructure development bolstering demand. While overseas investor sentiment, particularly from the Middle East, has seen a dip, end-user demand is resilient. Developers are navigating supply-side pressures on input costs, but the market's underlying strength continues to attract investment.

news.title: Lucknow development body gets 110 applications under OTS scheme
news.para: The Lucknow Development Authority (LDA) has received 110 applications for its one-time settlement scheme, aimed at resolving dues on properties. A help desk and camps are providing on-the-spot support for allottees seeking relief on defaulted installments and interest charges. The scheme, open until July 17, covers various property types.

news.title: People in 31 villages to get ownership rights of land in 'Lal Dora' area: South Delhi MP
news.para: Ownership rights for residential land in 31 Delhi villages are coming soon. Residents of these Lal Dora areas will receive property cards. This will allow them to secure bank loans easily. Drone mapping is complete. The Delhi government is finalizing regulations. This move addresses long-standing issues regarding land regularization.

news.title: SC issues notice in contempt petition concerning unauthorized constructions in Gurugram
news.para: The Supreme Court has issued a notice in a contempt petition filed by Rajdarbar Iconic Venture concerning unauthorized constructions in Gurgaon's Chakkarpur village. The petition alleges non-compliance with a December 2024 judgment that mandated obtaining completion certificates before providing civic services. Respondents, including senior Haryana officials, have been directed to submit replies by May 7, 2026.
